text.title: change-control Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for change-control as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: No formal process — changes happen informally via chat and verbal agreements.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Informal changes have no impact analysis, no audit trail, and no baseline update. They compound invisibly until the schedule or budget collapses with no documented cause.

failure-mode.description: PM approves every change regardless of size, bypassing Sponsor and CCB.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PM authority is limited by tier. Overreach on major changes removes the sponsor's ability to protect the project's ROI case and creates contractual exposure.

failure-mode.description: Implementing an approved CR without updating the scope, schedule, and budget baselines simultaneously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Baselines that lag behind approved changes make EVM calculations and future change impact analysis inaccurate. Update baselines atomically with implementation confirmation.

failure-mode.description: Rejecting changes without documenting the reason.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Undocumented rejections allow the same CR to re-appear under a different title. A rejection log with reasoning enables pattern detection and deflects repeat requests with evidence.
